proposal: We can save money by switching off all traffic lights. Red and green are expensive colors.

left.label: Keep them on
left.result_text: The lights stay on. Minister Penny begins calculating the cost of colors.

right.label: Switch them off
right.result_text: The Treasury saves money. Every intersection becomes a philosophical debate.

proposal: The people are grumpy. My niece suggests free pizza every Friday. She also owns a pizzeria.

options.label: No free lunch
options.result_text: No pizza. Citizens form a support group for disappointed stomachs.

options.label: Give them pizza
options.result_text: Citizens celebrate. The Treasury receives a very large cheese invoice.

proposal: Our happiness statistics look terrible. If everyone is legally required to smile, the statistics improve immediately.

left.label: That's absurd
left.result_text: Citizens keep their natural faces. Luna News sighs into her microphone.

right.label: Everyone smiles now
right.result_text: Smiles everywhere. Dentists report record profits and record suspicion.

proposal: My cat Barsik has strong opinions about local politics. Perhaps cats should be allowed to vote?

options.label: Cats can't vote
options.result_text: Democracy remains human-only. Barsik files a formal complaint by scratching the door.

options.label: Enfranchise the cats
options.result_text: Cats receive ballots. Voter turnout now depends on the weather and naps.

proposal: The army has not marched in weeks. A grand parade will remind everyone that we have exactly seven tanks.

options.label: No parade
options.result_text: No parade. General Boom polishes the tanks alone, sadly.

options.label: March!
options.result_text: Seven tanks roll proudly through the square. One takes a wrong turn.

proposal: Sunlight is free, but windows are not. I propose a modest tax on every window in the country.

left.label: Leave windows alone
left.result_text: Windows remain untaxed. Minister Penny eyes the doors thoughtfully.

right.label: Tax the windows
right.result_text: The tax passes. Bricklayers become the wealthiest profession overnight.

proposal: Studies show citizens do no work on weekends. Abolishing weekends would raise productivity by two sevenths.

left.label: Weekends stay
left.result_text: Weekends survive. Citizens celebrate by doing absolutely nothing.

right.label: Abolish weekends
right.result_text: Weekends are cancelled. Calendars nationwide enter a period of mourning.

proposal: Bad news makes people sad. I propose we only report good news. I have already prepared next month's weather.

left.label: Report the truth
left.result_text: The news stays honest. Ratings drop, but so does national confusion.

right.label: Only good news
right.result_text: Every broadcast is now delightful. Reality files a complaint that goes unreported.

proposal: An army marches on its stomach. I request a dedicated national snack budget for the troops.

left.label: No snacks
left.result_text: The troops receive standard rations and non-standard grumbling.

right.label: Fund the snacks
right.result_text: Morale soars. The army now guards the snack warehouse with unusual dedication.

proposal: CRISIS: The national budget spreadsheet caught fire. Figuratively, then literally. We need money by Friday.

options.label: Emergency tax on everything
options.result_text: Everything is taxed, including the tax forms. The budget survives; the mood does not.

options.label: Auction the palace furniture
options.result_text: The golden chairs sell instantly. Ministers now hold meetings standing up, furiously.

options.label: Print more money
options.result_text: The printer runs all night. Bread now costs a suspicious number of zeros.

proposal: Two ministers are arguing over who gets the larger office. Both have submitted formal complaints about the other.

left.label: Give it to neither
left.result_text: Both ministers become equally offended. Balance is restored.

right.label: Build another office
right.result_text: The government expands by one office and zero useful employees.
